risk_rationale: The Verifying Kids’ Online Privacy Act redefines 'child' as individuals under 16, which conflicts with TikTok's current baseline that defines the service as for age 13+. Additionally, the requirement for operators to verify the age of users introduces a compliance gap in TikTok's age assurance posture.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: TikTok states service is for age 13+ (or higher where local law requires)
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The current baseline allows users aged 13-15 to access the full service, which is inconsistent with the new definition of 'child' under the act.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: TikTok states it uses machine learning and safety review to identify likely under-13 users and remove/limit access
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Requires operators to verify the age of users accessing their services
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The current age assurance posture does not explicitly verify the age of all users, which is required by the new legislation.

remediations.title: Update Age Access Policies
remediations.description: Revise the age access policies to align with the new definition of 'child' as individuals under 16. Implement stricter controls for users aged 13-15 to ensure compliance.
acceptance_criteria: Age verification process implemented for all users

remediations.title: Implement Comprehensive Age Verification
remediations.description: Develop and integrate a robust age verification system to ensure compliance with the requirement to verify the age of users accessing the service.

business_impact: Failure to comply with the new legislation could result in significant fines and reputational damage, as well as potential restrictions on service availability for users under 16.
recommended_actions: Revise age-related policies to comply with new legislation
Develop and implement an age verification system
Conduct a compliance audit to ensure alignment with the new requirements
